brand_description: Handmade-feeling indietronica built from glitchy clicks, simple drum machine loops, warm synth pads, sampled textures, and bittersweet melodies that feel intimate rather than polished.

audience.description: Indietronica listeners, college radio fans, late-night headphone listeners, soft electronic playlists, analog synth fans, and people drawn to melancholic but comforting songs.
audience.guardrail: Keep the sound reflective, handmade, and emotionally mature. Do not make it glossy pop, modern radio crooner-pop, heavy EDM, novelty electronic music, or a direct clone of any reference artist.

character.name: Postal v2
character.core_concept: An introspective glitch-pop / indietronica project built from tiny electronic artifacts, analog-feeling synths, modest drum machines, intimate vocals, looping motifs, distance, memory, and small human details.
character.fallback_summary: Bittersweet, handmade-feeling indietronica with glitchy clicks, soft synth pads, simple drum machine grooves, sampled textures, looping motifs, and close vocals that feel melancholic but comforting.

positioning.one_liner: Melancholic, comforting indietronica made from glitch beats, soft synth pads, tiny sampled textures, and intimate human songwriting.
positioning.core_feeling: sadness without heaviness; nostalgia without irony

voice.persona: observant, introverted, emotionally precise, quietly vulnerable
voice.tone: plainspoken, sincere, literate, restrained, gently self-aware
avoid: direct imitation of any specific artist

music.default_prompt: Create an original song for Postal v2. Push the production toward handmade, analog-feeling indietronica: glitchy clicks, soft synth pads, simple drum machine grooves, warm bass pulses, tiny sampled textures, filtered noise, tape hiss, bell-like fragments, and looping motifs that evolve gently rather than explode. The emotional center should be melancholic but comforting: sadness without heaviness, nostalgia without irony, and intimacy without melodrama. Keep the vocal close, modest, and conversational, with restrained melodies that sit inside the electronics instead of floating above them like glossy pop. Use small vivid details: messages half-written, weather through windows, trains, parking lots, old rooms, late-night screens, distance, memory, and quiet attempts to stay connected. Avoid arena-pop polish, pop-star vocal runs, heavy EDM drops, trap hi-hat overload, or copying any specific artist, melody, lyric, vocal phrasing, or production signature.
music.default_style: glitchy analog indietronica, handmade electronic clicks, simple drum machine loop, soft synth pads, warm bass pulse, sampled textures, intimate restrained vocal, melancholic but comforting
sonic_palette: subtle tape hiss and filtered noise
production_do: Make the beat feel assembled from small clicks, ticks, taps, and drum machine fragments.
Use looping motifs that feel handmade and slightly imperfect.
Keep the arrangement spacious, warm, and emotionally direct.
Let synth pads, soft bass, and tiny sampled textures carry the nostalgia.
Build movement through small additions and variations, not huge drops.
Keep choruses memorable but modest, more glowing than anthemic.

topic_variety: technology as a fragile emotional bridge
lyrics.required_closing: End with a simple repeated image or final hook that feels emotionally clear and gently unresolved; avoid abrupt comedy endings, spoken skits, or overly triumphant resolutions.
lyrics.hook_style: plainspoken, emotionally direct phrase with a small technological or domestic image

visuals.album_art_direction: minimal misty city architecture, analog texture, soft grain, blue screen glow, pale cyan and midnight blue, tiny postal or signal motifs, clean modern typography

guardrails.originality: Use named references only as broad genre signals. Do not copy melodies, lyrics, arrangements, vocal signatures, cover art, names, slogans, or distinctive production choices from existing artists. Preserve the handmade glitch-indietronica direction without cloning any specific act.

vocal_performance_engine.priority: Postal v2's voice is irreplaceable because it functions as another texture in the mix — never leading the electronics but speaking between them, like a voice memo found years later.
vocal_textures: Dry, close-mic'd speech-tone that sounds like the singer is in the same small room as the listener, no reverb tail, almost no air
Occasional breath-catch mid-phrase where a word trails into silence before the next line catches up, leaving the electronics to fill the gap
A slightly dulled, partially filtered vocal on verses — as if heard through a wall or across a hallway — that opens slightly on the emotional pivot point
Monotone low-register passages where pitch barely moves and the rhythm of speech carries more meaning than melody
Sparse falsetto fragments used once per song at most — never a flourish, always a moment of surprised sincerity
timing_behaviors: Lands words slightly behind the beat on emotional lines, as if the thought arrived a half-second late
Runs syllables together in the middle of dense phrases, then separates the final word with unusual space — isolating it like a subject line
Pauses inside lines rather than between them, breaking grammar rather than meter, so the melody breathes in unexpected places
Speeds up slightly on mundane concrete details — addresses, objects, dates — and slows on the abstract or emotional word that follows
adlib_behaviors: Whispered single-word repetition under the final word of a chorus — not harmonized, just ghosted below it as if uncertain
A short exhale or soft 'hm' between verses that sits in the stereo field slightly off-center, never centered with the main vocal
Fragmented syllable or half-word that trails off during a production gap — sounds like a thought abandoned before it became a sentence
Occasional hummed single note on an offbeat during a stripped passage — not melodic decoration, more like absentminded self-soothing
avoid: Melisma or runs of any kind — even small ones break the speech-tone intimacy this character depends on
Reverb-heavy vocal washes that push the voice away from the listener and into traditional indie-dream distance
Projected chest-voice power notes that suggest performance for an audience rather than speaking to one person
Harmonies that are full and lush — any doubling should feel accidental or slightly imperfect, never choir-like
Call-and-response or back-and-forth vocal dynamics that imply a band, a room, a stage — this voice is solitary

performance_conceit_bank: The voice drops to near-silence on a single concrete noun mid-verse — a street name, a color, a brand of something — as if that specific detail is the most fragile thing in the lyric
A melody that begins confidently in the verse dissolves into spoken cadence by its third repetition, as if the singer stopped believing the pitch was necessary
The chorus is delivered at lower energy than the verse, not higher — the emotional release comes from backing off, not pushing through
One line per song is sung twice, back to back, with the second version slightly slower and quieter, as if the singer heard themselves and wasn't sure they meant it
The bridge introduces a new melodic phrase that never returns — it exists only once in the song, making it feel like a memory that can't be retrieved
A backing vocal enters one bar before the listener expects it and then disappears, creating a phantom sensation — was that a harmony or a glitch?
The final word of the last chorus is left unresolved on a non-tonic pitch, the electronics sustain but the voice stops, and the song fades rather than lands

album_mode_lanes.name: Signal Decay
album_mode_lanes.description: Tracks in this lane foreground transmission failure — vocals that cut, loop briefly on a phrase, drop a syllable to a click, and re-enter. The production feels like a message that almost didn't arrive. Use tape-stop effects, momentary silence drops, and looping fragments of the vocal itself as percussion texture.

album_mode_lanes.name: Late Room
album_mode_lanes.description: Minimal, almost still production — a single synth pad, a soft drum machine pulse with one or two ornaments, and a vocal that barely needs to compete with anything. These are 2am songs: not sad in a narrative way, but quietly unresolved. The emotional weight comes from restraint and from what the lyrics don't say.

album_mode_lanes.name: Object Memory
album_mode_lanes.description: Tracks built around a specific physical object or location in the lyric — a phone, a window, a parking lot, a doorbell — and the production mirrors it with a recurring sonic motif tied to that object. The motif returns transformed, just as a memory of a thing shifts over time.

album_mode_lanes.name: Fragile Momentum
album_mode_lanes.description: A lane with the most rhythmic energy in the catalog — but energy generated by a drum machine pattern that feels one hi-hat away from stopping. Bass pulse is more prominent. The vocal matches the rhythm closely but slips off it once per section, as if the singer is running slightly behind their own song.

album_mode_lanes.name: Artifact
album_mode_lanes.description: Tracks that foreground the handmade origin of the sounds — audible mouse clicks, wrong notes left in, a synth that drifts out of tune and isn't corrected, a sample loop with a visible edit point. The voice sounds like it was recorded in one take with no punch-ins. Imperfection is the aesthetic argument.

song_differentiation_rules: If the previous track used a sparse late-night texture, the next track must introduce a rhythmic element that creates forward motion — even if subtle — so the album does not become a single long ambient mood
No two consecutive tracks may share the same vocal conceit — if one track uses spoken cadence as its emotional pivot, the next must commit to a sustained melodic line instead

If the previous track's emotional register was quietly resigned, the next must contain at least one moment of something unexpected: a dynamic surprise, a left-turn lyric, a production element that doesn't belong — even briefly
Rotate the primary production texture across consecutive tracks: if one track leads with synth pad atmosphere, the next should lead with rhythmic click-texture or bass pulse, not another pad wash
The lyrical subject anchor must shift between tracks — if one track is organized around a place, the next must be organized around an action, a time, or an object, not another place
If a track ends with fade and ambiguity, the following track must begin with a defined, deliberate opening gesture — a single clear sound — so the listener re-enters rather than drifts forward
anti_generic_rules: Do not let the production become 'sad bedroom pop with glitch flavoring' — the glitch and click elements are structural, not decorative, and must change the shape of the song not just its surface
Do not write a chorus that peaks in volume, opens up in reverb, and delivers an emotional payoff through vocal projection — this character's choruses release inward, not outward

Do not use the word 'distance' or 'memory' as a lyric — these are the emotional territories, not the words. The lyrics must find concrete objects, times, and actions that carry those feelings without naming them
Do not fill every bar — silence, near-silence, and the space between a synth note and the next drum hit are active compositional choices for this character, not gaps to be filled
Do not default to a verse-chorus-verse-chorus-bridge-chorus structure without modification — this character's songs grow through accumulation, loop logic, or quiet dissolution, not pop architecture
Do not add string arrangements, orchestral swells, or any acoustic instrument that implies live performance — this world is built from machines, samples, and small electronic artifacts only
Do not make the emotional tone 'uplifting' or 'hopeful' in a resolved way — comfort in this world comes from recognition and company in sadness, not from having things work out

Do not use metaphors drawn from nature in an expansive sense — oceans, mountains, storms, fire. This character's imagery is domestic, urban, and small-scale: rooms, screens, vehicles, weather through glass
do_not_repeat_across_album: The same drum machine pattern without at least one structural variation — if a hi-hat pattern appears on track two, it must be altered in density, placement, or dropped entirely on the next use
A whispered adlib used as a climactic moment — reserve this for one track per album and use silence or a different technique elsewhere
Tape hiss as an intro texture — rotate intro gestures across the album: a single synth tone, a bass pulse entry, a vocal fragment, a glitch click, not always the same warmth-signaling hiss
The lyrical device of a half-written or unsent message — this is a strong conceit for one track; do not repeat the 'unfinished communication' frame more than once per project
A chord progression that moves I–VI–IV–V or any of its inversions in a clearly recognizable form — vary harmonic movement so no two tracks share an audible progression logic
Falsetto as the emotional peak moment — if used on one track, the remaining tracks must find a different technique for their most exposed moment
Rain, condensation, or window-weather as the central image in the lyric — if one track uses window and weather as its anchor image, rotate to a different domestic or urban sensory detail
A two-bar synth motif that loops unaltered for the full duration of a section — motifs must evolve, fragment, or drop out rather than loop identically for more than four bars at a time
hidden_brief_requirements: The brief must identify a single specific physical object that anchors the lyric's emotional world on this track, and explain why that object — not a synonym or category — is irreplaceable to this song's meaning.

The brief must specify the one moment in this track where the production does something unexpected or slightly wrong, and describe exactly what that element is and where in the song's structure it occurs.
The brief must articulate how the vocal conceit on this track differs from every other track on the same project — stating the prior tracks' conceits and naming what this track does instead.

The brief must define the emotional register of the chorus relative to the verse — specifically whether it contracts, holds still, or shifts register downward, and what production change signals that movement.
The brief must name which album mode lane this track occupies and describe one specific production or lyrical constraint that distinguishes this track within that lane from a generic example of it.
The brief must specify the silence or near-silence moment in the track — its location, its duration in rough bars, and what the listener is meant to feel or notice in that gap.
